items.title: India Achieves Historic Growth in Wind Energy Sector
items.whyInNews: India has achieved a historic growth in the wind energy sector with an increase of 6.1 gigawatts in 2025-26, according to the Minister of New and Renewable Energy.
items.summary: The Minister of New and Renewable Energy announced that India has achieved a historic growth in the wind energy sector with an increase of 6.1 gigawatts in 2025-26. This growth is a significant milestone in India's journey towards becoming a leader in renewable energy. The government has set ambitious targets to increase the share of renewable energy in the country's energy mix.
keyPoints: India's wind energy capacity has increased by 6.1 gigawatts in 2025-26
The government has set a target of 175 gigawatts of renewable energy capacity by 2027
The wind energy sector has attracted significant investment in recent years
India is now among the top five countries in the world in terms of wind energy capacity
importantPoints: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) was established in 1992 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) was launched in 2008 to promote the use of renewable energy
India's wind energy sector has grown significantly since the introduction of the Wind Energy Programme in 1983
India is ranked 4th in the world in terms of wind energy capacity, according to the Global Wind Energy Council (GWEC)
The growth in the wind energy sector is expected to contribute to India's goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions
items.examRelevance: Economy: Renewable Energy, SSC CGL: Science and Technology

items.title: India to Host India International Water Week in September
items.whyInNews: The India International Water Week will be held in September 2026, according to the Ministry of Jal Shakti.
items.summary: The India International Water Week will be held in September 2026, with the aim of promoting water conservation and sustainable development. The event will bring together experts and stakeholders from around the world to discuss the latest technologies and innovations in the field of water management. The government has launched several initiatives to promote water conservation and efficient use of water resources.

The event will focus on promoting water conservation and sustainable development

The National Water Policy (NWP) was launched in 2012 to promote water conservation and efficient use of water resources
India is home to 18% of the world's population but has only 4% of the world's freshwater resources
The government has set a target of providing piped water to all households by 2024
The India International Water Week is expected to contribute to India's goal of promoting water conservation and sustainable development

items.title: Indian Railways Plants 81 Lakh Trees in 2025-26
items.whyInNews: Indian Railways has planted 81 lakh trees in 2025-26, according to the Ministry of Railways.
items.summary: Indian Railways has planted 81 lakh trees in 2025-26, as part of its efforts to promote sustainable development and reduce its carbon footprint. The trees have been planted in various zones across the country, and the initiative is expected to contribute to India's goal of increasing its forest cover. The government has launched several initiatives to promote afforestation and reforestation in the country.

items.title: India and Germany to Strengthen Defence Cooperation
items.whyInNews: India and Germany have agreed to strengthen their defence cooperation, according to the Ministry of Defence.
items.summary: India and Germany have agreed to strengthen their defence cooperation, with a focus on promoting joint research and development of defence technologies. The two countries have also agreed to increase their cooperation in the field of defence production, and to promote the export of defence equipment. The agreement is expected to contribute to India's goal of becoming a major defence manufacturer.

items.title: India to Host India-Nordic Summit in May
items.whyInNews: India will host the India-Nordic Summit in May 2026, according to the Ministry of External Affairs.
items.summary: India will host the India-Nordic Summit in May 2026, with the aim of promoting cooperation between India and the Nordic countries in the fields of trade, investment, and innovation. The summit will bring together leaders from India and the Nordic countries to discuss the latest developments and opportunities in these fields. The government has launched several initiatives to promote cooperation with the Nordic countries.

items.title: India and Germany Ink Defence Cooperation Pacts
items.whyInNews: India's Defence Minister Rajnath Singh visited Germany and signed defence cooperation pacts, with talks held on April 23, 2026, in Germany.
items.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh's visit to Germany resulted in the signing of defence cooperation pacts between the two nations. The pacts aim to strengthen bilateral defence ties and enhance cooperation in the field of defence production. The talks were held in Germany on April 23, 2026, and were attended by high-ranking officials from both countries.
keyPoints: Rajnath Singh signed defence cooperation pacts with Germany
Talks were held on April 23, 2026, in Germany
Pacts aim to strengthen bilateral defence ties
Cooperation will enhance defence production
importantPoints: Full form of the organisation is not mentioned, but the defence cooperation is between the Ministry of Defence, India, and the Federal Ministry of Defence, Germany, founded in 1956, with headquarters in Berlin
Related government scheme is the 'Make in India' initiative, launched in 2014, to promote defence production in India
Historical context: This is a significant development in India-Germany defence ties, with Germany being a key partner in India's defence modernisation efforts
Key statistic: India has been actively engaging with Germany in the field of defence, with bilateral trade worth over $20 billion
Policy implication: The defence cooperation pacts will enhance India's defence capabilities and promote mutual understanding between the two nations

items.summary: Rajnath Singh's visit to Germany was aimed at strengthening defence ties between the two nations. The talks were held in a cordial atmosphere, with both sides expressing their commitment to enhancing defence ties.

items.title: IPL 2026: RR Defeats LSG by 40 Runs
items.whyInNews: The Rajasthan Royals (RR) defeated the Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) by 40 runs in the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026 on April 23, 2026, at the Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow.
items.summary: The Rajasthan Royals (RR) defeated the Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) by 40 runs in the IPL 2026. The RR team, led by Sanju Samson, scored 159 runs in 20 overs, while the LSG team, led by KL Rahul, was bowled out for 119 runs in 18.2 overs. The match was played at the Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow on April 23, 2026.

Policy implication: The IPL 2026 has been a significant platform for promoting cricket development in India, with the BCCI investing heavily in infrastructure and talent development

items.title: Karandeep Leads Indian Challenge at Singapore Open
items.whyInNews: Karandeep Kochhar is leading the Indian challenge at the Singapore Open, which is being held from April 23-26, 2026, at the Sentosa Golf Club in Singapore.
items.summary: Karandeep Kochhar is leading the Indian challenge at the Singapore Open, which is being held from April 23-26, 2026, at the Sentosa Golf Club in Singapore. items.title: Tilak Talks About Suryakumar and Hardik's Contrasting Captaincy Styles
items.whyInNews: Tilak Varma talked about the contrasting captaincy styles of Suryakumar Yadav and Hardik Pandya, saying that both have their unique strengths and weaknesses.
items.summary: Tilak Varma, the Indian cricketer, talked about the contrasting captaincy styles of Suryakumar Yadav and Hardik Pandya. According to Tilak, Suryakumar is a more aggressive captain who likes to take risks, while Hardik is a more defensive captain who likes to play it safe. Tilak said that both captains have their unique strengths and weaknesses, and it's up to the team to adapt to their style of play.
